steps.explanation: The total weight equals the weight per cubic foot times the volume: \(x = 160 \times V\), where \(V\) is the volume in cubic feet.
The volume satisfies \(14.6 < V < 18.6\). Multiplying all parts of the inequality by 160 (positive, so direction is preserved): \(160(14.6) < x < 160(18.6)\).

Evaluate each choice:
  • \(18.6 < x < 160 \times 14.6\): Lower and upper bounds are swapped and inconsistently scaled. Eliminated.
  • \(14.6 < x < 160 \times 18.6\): The left bound is a raw volume value, not a weight. Eliminated.
  • \(160(14.6) < x < 160(18.6)\): Both bounds correctly scale the volume range by the weight density. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • \(\frac{160}{18.6} < x < \frac{160}{14.6}\): Divides instead of multiplies; produces weight per unit volume, not total weight. Eliminated.
